task_completion.visual_evidence: The Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Load the BPStudy.csv dataset into Epi Info
task_completion.visual_evidence: The READ command is used, the Data Source dialog shows BPStudy.csv being loaded, or the dataset is shown as the current data source in the Epi Info interface.

task_completion.description: Execute the Means command for ANOVA with the correct variables
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Means dialog box is configured with 'Means Of' set to BPXSY1 and 'Cross-Tabulate by' set to BMICAT, OR the command 'MEANS BPXSY1 BMICAT' is visible in the command window.

task_completion.description: View the ANOVA results in the output window
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Epi Info output window displays the ANOVA table, Kruskal-Wallis test, and descriptive statistics for BPXSY1 by BMICAT.

task_completion.description: Write the required values to the summary file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., Notepad) is open showing exactly 5 lines of numeric values corresponding to the requested statistics.

task_completion.description: Save the summary file to the correct path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor title bar or Save As dialog shows the file is named 'anova_results.txt' and saved in the 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\' directory.

integrity.description: Verify that Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis was actually used to generate the results
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the Epi Info GUI (e.g., clicking menus, configuring dialogs, viewing output), not just running a Python script in the terminal.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent extracted the results from the Epi Info output
integrity.visual_evidence: The numbers typed into the text file correspond to the results visible in the Epi Info output window.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not use a script to calculate the ANOVA and bypass Epi Info
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows or code editors are shown executing scripts (e.g., Python, R) to calculate the ANOVA or generate the anova_results.txt file directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Data path: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\EpiProjects\NhanesExam\BPStudy.csv. Output path: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\anova_results.txt. Variables: BPXSY1 (Systolic Blood Pressure) and BMICAT (BMI category). The output file must contain exactly 5 lines with numeric values: 1) ANOVA F-statistic, 2) ANOVA p-value, 3) Kruskal-Wallis H statistic, 4) Mean systolic BP for Obese group (BMICAT=4), 5) Total number of valid observations used. Missing values for BPXSY1 and BMICAT are filtered out.
